html:
Exclusive to:

Wildbore Houses replace Pig Houses in the Shipwrecked DLC. They are occasionally encountered in the Beach biome. Each house contains a single Wildbore, which will roam around the house during day time. A new Wildbore will spawn from the house in 4 days, if the previous occupant is killed. Similar to Pig Houses, Wildbore Houses emit light at night if they are occupied, but it will go out if the player gets too close to the house. The houses can be crafted using 8 Bamboos, 5 Palm Leaves, and 4 Pig Skins, and requires the Alchemy Engine to prototype.

Similar to Pigs, Wildbores will always head to their houses at dusk, unless they are in combat when the day changes to dusk. Unlike Pigs, Wildbores will attempt to protect their houses if they see the player destroying it with a Hammer.

If a Shipwrecked-compatible world is created, there is a small chance of having several Wildbore Houses spawn within a Pig Village, making them accessible in worlds that have not been connected to a Shipwrecked world.

Trivia

  • In the pre-release version of Shipwrecked, Strong Winds could destroy Wildbore Houses. This behavior has been removed from the game in the Seas the Day update.

Gallery

  • A burnt Wildbore House.
  • A group of Wildbore Houses in a Reign of Giants world.

"wildborehouse"
“What a bore-ing house.”
–Wilson
“Add fire and you'd get BBQ!”
–Willow
“Puny house could not hold me.”
–Wolfgang
“Hopefully they won't invite me in.”
–Wendy
“HOW WILD CAN THEY BE IF THEY LIVE IN A HOUSE?”
–WX-78
“A shelter for the bore.”
–Wickerbottom
“Looks like a house fulla hosers.”
–Woodie
“Is this what passes for architecture around here?”
–Maxwell
“Rudimentary, but quite sound structurally.”
–Wagstaff
“A terrible place tö live!”
–Wigfrid
“Muffled oinking is coming from inside.”
–Webber
“Bore's live in houses? Wild!”
–Walani
“How wild can they be if they live in houses?”
–Warly
“Bet them bores be knowin' how ta parrrrrty.”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS THE HOUSE OF WILDBORE”
–Wilba
“Anyone home?”
–Wormwood
“I'd rather live on my balloon.”
–Wheeler
Wildbore Houses replace Pig Houses in the Shipwrecked DLC. They are occasionally encountered in the Beach biome. Each house contains a single Wildbore, which will roam around the house during day time. A new Wildbore will spawn from the house in 4 days, if the previous occupant is killed. Similar to Pig Houses, Wildbore Houses emit light at night if they are occupied, but it will go out if the player gets too close to the house. The houses can be crafted using 8 Bamboos, 5 Palm Leaves, and 4 Pig Skins, and requires the Alchemy Engine to prototype.
Similar to Pigs, Wildbores will always head to their houses at dusk, unless they are in combat when the day changes to dusk. Unlike Pigs, Wildbores will attempt to protect their houses if they see the player destroying it with a Hammer.
If a Shipwrecked-compatible world is created, there is a small chance of having several Wildbore Houses spawn within a Pig Village, making them accessible in worlds that have not been connected to a Shipwrecked world.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
In the pre-release version of Shipwrecked, Strong Winds could destroy Wildbore Houses. This behavior has been removed from the game in the Seas the Day update.

'''Wildbore Houses''' replace [[Pig Houses]] in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC. They are occasionally encountered in the [[Beach]] [[biome]]. Each house contains a single [[Wildbore]], which will roam around the house during day time. A new Wildbore will spawn from the house in 4 days, if the previous occupant is killed. Similar to Pig Houses, Wildbore Houses emit light at night if they are occupied, but it will go out if the player gets too close to the house. The houses can be crafted using 8 [[Bamboo]]s, 5 [[Palm Leaf|Palm Leaves]], and 4 [[Pig Skin]]s, and requires the [[Alchemy Engine]] to prototype.

Similar to [[Pigs]], Wildbores will always head to their houses at dusk, unless they are in combat when the day changes to dusk. Unlike Pigs, Wildbores will attempt to protect their houses if they see the player destroying it with a [[Hammer]].

If a ''Shipwrecked''-compatible world is created, there is a small chance of having several Wildbore Houses spawn within a [[Pig Village]], making them accessible in worlds that have not been connected to a ''Shipwrecked'' world.

== {{Pic32|placeholder}} Trivia ==
* In the pre-release version of ''Shipwrecked'', [[Strong Winds]] could destroy Wildbore Houses. This behavior has been removed from the game in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked#Version History|Seas the Day]]'' update.
